zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Hyde於1565年出生在英國英格蘭爾斯托普特時，她的父親Sir Robert Hyde22歲，母親Beatrice Calverley20歲。她於1587年12月12日與Edward Vawdrey在英國英格蘭爾斯托普特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1615年享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Hyde于1565年出生在英国英格兰爾斯托普特时，她的父亲Sir Robert Hyde22岁，母亲Beatrice Calverley20岁。她于1587年12月12日与Edward Vawdrey在英国英格兰爾斯托普特结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1615年享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of various places so called in Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Hampshire, and Middlesex. They were named with Old English hīd ‘hide (of land)’, a variable measure of land, differing from place to place and time to time, and seems to have been originally fixed as the amount necessary to support one (extended) family (Old English hīgan, hīwan ‘household’). The surname may also be topographic for someone living on (and farming) a hide of land. The Hyde family has been in Leinster, Ireland, since the early 13th century and one family has been established in the county of Cork since the 16th century. The name was Gaelicized as both Dalaithíd and de hÍde. Compare Hyder .

variants.name: Dollarhide
meanings: Irish: possibly an altered form of de la Hyde (see Hyde ), which was a prominent surname in Leinster from medieval times onward. Compare Dollahite .

History: Francis Dollahide, born c. 1650–60 in County Dublin, Ireland, was the ancestor of Americans with the surname Dollahide, Dollahite, and Dollarhide. In 1680 he was transported to MD for some youthful misdemeanor. He appears to have been an educated man and rose to be an important landowner and politician, serving in the Lower House of the MD legislature as an elected representative for Baltimore County from c. 1705 to 1721.

variants.name: Hyder
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): status name or topographic name for one who lived on or possessed a hide of land, from Middle English hide + the locative suffix -er. This is a characteristically southeastern English name, synonymous with Middle English atte hide elsewhere (see Hyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Hyde was born in 1565, in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert Hyde, was 22 and her mother, Beatrice Calverley, was 20. She married Edward Vawdrey on 12 December 1587, in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 1615, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Hyde est née en 1565, à Stockport, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Hyde, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Calverley, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Edward Vawdrey le 12 décembre 1587, à Stockport, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1615, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Hyde 1565 in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Hyde 22 und ihre Mutter Beatrice Calverley 20. Sie hat am 12. Dezember 1587 in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Edward Vawdrey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb um 1615 im Alter von 51 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hyde è nata il nel 1565 nel Stockport, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Robert Hyde, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Beatrice Calverley, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Edward Vawdrey il 12 dicembre 1587, nel Stockport, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Morì circa 1615, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hydeが1565年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアストクプオルトで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Hydeは22歳，母Beatrice Calverleyは20歳であった。1587年12月12日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアストクプオルトでEdward Vawdreyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1615年に51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hyde(이)가 1565년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스톡포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Hyde(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Calverley(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1587년 12월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스톡포트에서 Edward Vawdrey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1615년 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hyde nasceu em 1565, em Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Robert Hyde, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice Calverley, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Edward Vawdrey em 12 de dezembro de 1587, em Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1615, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Hyde родилась в 1565 г. в местности стоккпорт, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Hyde было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Calverley, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward Vawdrey 12 декабря 1587 г. в местности стоккпорт, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла около 1615 г. в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Hyde nació en 1565, en Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Robert Hyde, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Beatrice Calverley, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Edward Vawdrey el 12 de diciembre de 1587, en Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1615, a la edad de 51 años.
